assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Line 7 of claude-md-result.md: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' — matches the reference values exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: Line 8 of claude-md-result.md: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — trustify-ui is present with serena_ui as the Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 12-16 of claude-md-result.md show: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747 — all match reference values exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has two clearly labeled sections: '## Preserved (unchanged from existing configuration)' listing trustify-backend, Jira Configuration, Code Intelligence, and Bug Configuration; and '## Newly Added' listing the trustify-ui Repository Registry entry and serena_ui limitations entry.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 5: 'serena_backend: Already configured in Repository Registry (trustify-backend, Rust backend service, /home/user/trustify-backend). No changes needed.' Line 6: 'serena_ui: Newly discovered from MCP tool listing. Not yet present in Repository Registry.'

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 20-29 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name'), the mcp__serena_backend__find_symbol example, and line 33 preserves the serena_backend limitation ('rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). Only line 34 is new: 'serena_ui: No known limitations'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing '## Bug Configuration' section with Bug issue type ID (10001), Bug template (docs/bug-template.md), and Bug-to-Task link type (Blocks) unchanged — the skill reports Bug Configuration is up to date
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 38-40: Bug issue type ID: 10001, Bug template: docs/bug-template.md, Bug-to-Task link type: Blocks — all match reference values. discovery-log.md confirms: 'Already present with all required fields... No changes needed.'

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 18: 'User declined to enable security triage. Section not added.' changes-log.md line 25: 'Security Configuration: User declined to enable security triage; section omitted.' claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section — the file ends after Bug Configuration at line 40.
